title.emphasis: The Foundational Layer: The LLC Operating Agreement

section.para: The single most important document for the venture is the LLC Operating Agreement. This is the internal constitution that binds the members and governs how the company operates. It is not merely a formality but the core legal framework that makes the entire talent-escalation pathway possible. To function as intended, the Operating Agreement must be custom-drafted with several critical provisions from the outset.

listitem.para: Management Structure: The agreement should establish a "manager-managed" LLC, legally codifying the role of the patron as the "executive author" with ultimate decision-making authority.
Process for Admitting New Members: It must clearly define the procedure for adding new members, ensuring a smooth and legally sound transition from contractor to partner.

Intellectual Property (IP) as Capital Contribution: The agreement must explicitly state that members can make non-cash capital contributions, specifically including intellectual property. This is the mechanism that allows an artist's work to be formally recognized as their investment in the company.
IP Ownership: The agreement should clarify that any IP created by members in the course of business belongs to the LLC, preventing future ownership disputes.
